articles.description: Research Highlight - Self-Sovereign Agent, Agentic AI Summit Early Bird Tickets, AgentX–AgentBeats Phase 2, Sprint 3 Begins, and Lambda Custom Track Winners

articles.title: Hackers break into Claude Mythos: OpenAI launches a security-specialized competitor to Mythos
articles.description: Maine votes to pause construction of data centers. Journalists at major U.S. newspapers push back on publisher’s demand to use AI. AI-generated talking-head videos that support President Trump flood social media. Humanoid robots outrun human world record in a half-marathon race.

articles.title: Making ChatGPT better for clinicians
articles.description: OpenAI makes ChatGPT for Clinicians free for verified U.S. physicians, nurse practitioners, and pharmacists, supporting clinical care, documentation, and research.

articles.title: Workspace agents
articles.description: Learn how to build, use, and scale workspace agents in ChatGPT to automate repeatable workflows, connect tools, and streamline team operations.

articles.title: Introducing workspace agents in ChatGPT
articles.description: Workspace agents in ChatGPT are Codex-powered agents that automate complex workflows, run in the cloud, and help teams scale work across tools securely.

articles.title: Speeding up agentic workflows with WebSockets in the Responses API
articles.description: A deep dive into the Codex agent loop, showing how WebSockets and connection-scoped caching reduced API overhead and improved model latency.

articles.title: Introducing OpenAI Privacy Filter
articles.description: OpenAI Privacy Filter is an open-weight model for detecting and redacting personally identifiable information (PII) in text with state-of-the-art accuracy

articles.title: We're launching two specialized TPUs for the agentic era.
articles.description: The eighth generation of Google’s TPU includes two specialized chips that will power the future of AI.

articles.title:
Workspace agents can work across tools—pulling context from docs, email, chats, code, and systems, and taking approved actions like updating @Linear issues, creating docs, or sending messages.

In @Sl
articles.description:
Workspace agents can work across tools—pulling context from docs, email, chats, code, and systems, and taking approved actions like updating @Linear issues, creating docs, or sending messages.

In @SlackHQ, agents can jump into a thread, understand what’s needed, pull the right context, help resolve the issue, and update the right systems.

They can also keep going in the background or on a schedule, while you’re away.

articles.title:
🚀 Meet Qwen3.6-27B, our latest dense, open-source model, packing flagship-level coding power!

Yes, 27B, and Qwen3.6-27B punches way above its weight. 👇

What's new:
🧠 Outstanding agentic coding —
articles.description:
🚀 Meet Qwen3.6-27B, our latest dense, open-source model, packing flagship-level coding power!

Yes, 27B, and Qwen3.6-27B punches way above its weight. 👇

What's new:
🧠 Outstanding agentic coding — surpasses Qwen3.5-397B-A17B across all major coding benchmarks
💡 Strong reasoning across text & multimodal tasks
🔄 Supports thinking & non-thinking modes
✅ Apache 2.0 — fully open, fully yours

Smaller model. Bigger results. Community's favorite. ❤️
We can't wait to see what you build with Qwen3.6-27B! 👀

🔗👇
Blog: https://t.co/P2Zx7FwMxB
Qwen Studio: https://t.co/c4vm4LuZrU
Github: https://t.co/zKDEbv0R4U
Hugging Face: 
https://t.co/N67hyzxvfr
https://t.co/SSdtbWRDap
ModelScope:
https://t.co/xODf1pj9kw
https://t.co/xXhoqlJ2AB

articles.title: LM Performance：With only 27B parameters, Qwen3.6-27B outperforms the Qwen3.5-397B-A17B (397B total / 17B active, ~15x larger!) on every major coding benchmark — including SWE-bench Verified (77.2 vs.
articles.description: LM Performance：With only 27B parameters, Qwen3.6-27B outperforms the Qwen3.5-397B-A17B (397B total / 17B active, ~15x larger!) on every major coding benchmark — including SWE-bench Verified (77.2 vs. 76.2), SWE-bench Pro (53.5 vs. 50.9), Terminal-Bench 2.0 (59.3 vs. 52.5), and SkillsBench (48.2 vs. 30.0). It also surpasses all peer-scale dense models by a wide margin.

articles.title: VLM Performance：Qwen3.6-27B is natively multimodal, supporting both vision-language thinking and non-thinking modes in a single unified checkpoint — the same as Qwen3.6-35B-A3B. It handles images and
articles.description: VLM Performance：Qwen3.6-27B is natively multimodal, supporting both vision-language thinking and non-thinking modes in a single unified checkpoint — the same as Qwen3.6-35B-A3B. It handles images and video alongside text, enabling multimodal reasoning, document understanding, and visual question answering.
